finding: Permission leaf widened enum→`string`; evaluator user narrowed `User`→`PermissionUser={is_admin?}`. Widening an INPUT leaf only accepts more (Permissions enum members assignable to string); a full User is structurally assignable to {is_admin?}. App-shim `core/permissions/types.ts` re-narrows `Permission=Permissions` so ziee keeps enum type-safety. hasPermission's is_admin bypass + `*` + `a::b::*` hierarchical wildcard copied byte-for-byte. framework tsc=0, ui tsc=0, desktop tsc=0.

The gating semantics are security-load-bearing and UNCHANGED: order is (1) user.is_admin root bypass, (2) exact match, (3) `*` global, (4) hierarchical `::` wildcard — mirrors backend permissions/checker.rs. `<Can>`/usePermission fail-closed (fallback default null; empty-permissions→false; null expr→false). authStoreProxy reads the SAME app-registered Auth store the refetch endpoints are gated by, so a client can't widen its own grants. No permission string is trusted from the wire — evaluated against the /auth/me-sourced permissions[].

PROVEN equivalent: `useAuthStore = Auth.store` (Auth.store.ts:830) and Stores.Auth is the proxy over that same store, so hasPermissionNow's new `Stores.Auth.$` ≡ old `useAuthStore.getState()` (snapshot), and usePermission's `Stores.Auth.{user,permissions}` reactive destructure ≡ old reactive read. usePermission stays reactive (proxy subscribe), hasPermissionNow stays non-reactive (.$). No behavior change.

Chose a typed local CAST on the framework's own Stores over an injected `configurePermissions(useHook)` DI: the runtime read is byte-identical to the pre-move code, and a static cast can't violate rules-of-hooks the way a swappable injected hook could (a null→app-hook swap mid-tree would change the hook order). usePermission calls no conditional hooks; the proxy read is the only subscription.

FE-8 PROVEN: `grep -rniE 'platejs|@platejs|streamdown|codemirror|@codemirror|shiki|mermaid|pdfjs' @ziee/shell/src` = ZERO matches. The shell's entire non-relative dep surface is react/react-use/overlayscrollbars-react/@ziee/framework/*/@ziee/kit; @ziee/kit/package.json carries none of the 6 heavy deps. The 9 heavy domain-widget components (BUCKET-C-1) had NO inbound edge from any moved shell/permission file, so severance needed zero control-inversion.

gallery runtime-health pass (43 pages ×3 states + 47 overlays + 17 deep + 99 seeded = 200 cells ×2 themes) against the rewired tree: 363 findings, HIGH 0 gating / MEDIUM 181 / LOW 182, 0 surface(s) with gating HIGH. The ThemeProvider (root wrapper of every surface), per-module error boundaries, and blank layout render healthy — no NEW HIGH vs the pre-existing informational baseline. RUNTIME_FINDINGS.md git-restored after.

The desktop localOverridePlugin resolves `.desktop.tsx` variants ONLY for `@/`-prefixed specifiers. app-layout (LeftSidebar/Drawer/HeaderBarContainer/SidebarToggleButton .desktop.tsx) + settings (SettingsPage.desktop.tsx) carry platform variants — moving them into @ziee/shell (relative internal imports) would silently drop the desktop override → NOT moved (BOUNDARY B-1). Every file in the shell move-set was grep-verified to have ZERO `.desktop.*` sibling, so the extraction is desktop-safe. desktop/ui tsc=0.

Every CUT BUCKET-A/B dest exists: shell src has theme/{ThemeProvider,accentPresets,resolveTheme,useTheme,themeColor}, error/AppErrorBoundary, components/{Loading,LazyComponentRenderer,DivScrollY}, layouts/BlankLayout, bootstrap/AppShell, hooks/usePrefetchModules; framework has permissions/{types,hasPermission,evaluatePermission,authView,usePermission,hasPermissionNow,Can,index}. shell tsc=0 + framework tsc=0 resolve every symbol. Sibling @ziee/{kit,gallery} tsc=0 (framework change safe).

Every moved file's old `@/` path is a byte-thin re-export SHIM (not a divergent copy): 7 core/permissions shims (all 7 kept — deep imports @/core/permissions/{Can,usePermission} exist), 5 ThemeProvider shims, hooks/{useTheme,usePrefetchModules}, AppErrorBoundary, core/components/{Loading,LazyComponentRenderer}, common/DivScrollY, layouts/blank/BlankLayout. ziee's ~hundreds of `@/`-importers + the glob module discovery + the desktop override plugin are untouched. App.tsx is the only non-shim rewrite (thin AppShell consumer).

@ziee/shell added to BOTH ui/tsconfig.json and desktop/ui/tsconfig.json paths (`@ziee/shell` + `@ziee/shell/*` → sdk/packages/shell/src). node_modules/@ziee/shell symlink present (npm workspace). @ziee/framework/package.json gains `"./permissions"` export. shell tsconfig mirrors gallery (paths for @ziee/{kit,framework} + env.d.ts `declare module '*.css'`). All 4 tsc gates (framework/shell/ui/desktop) = 0.
Pure frontend. git status shows ZERO changes to any api-client/types.ts (ui + desktop), openapi.json, or Rust file. No OpenAPI regen needed; the golden types.ts parity test is unaffected.

Baseline framework tsc was RED (pre-existing, unrelated): router/config.test.ts's inline RoutePermissionGate gave `children: unknown` (not ReactNode). Fixed the one-line test annotation to `children: import('react').ReactNode` so the framework-tsc=0 gate for the NEW permissions subpath is meetable. Disclosed; not scope-creep beyond enabling the gate.
